title: World Biome Map
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive world map showing seven terrestrial biomes with hover details for temperature, precipitation, and representative organisms

learningObjectives: Classify and locate the seven major terrestrial biomes on a world map
Describe the temperature range, precipitation, and representative organisms of each biome
Explain the relationship between latitude, climate, and biome distribution
Compare the geographic extent of different biomes across continents
prerequisites: Basic understanding of climate (temperature and precipitation)
Knowledge of what an ecosystem is

keyQuestions: Why do biomes form in roughly latitudinal bands around the Earth?
What two climate factors most determine which biome develops in a location?
Why do similar biomes exist on different continents despite being separated by oceans?
How might climate change shift biome boundaries?
commonMisconceptions: Biome boundaries are sharp lines (in reality they are gradual transitions called ecotones)
Temperature alone determines biome type (precipitation is equally important)
Each continent has unique biomes (similar biomes appear on multiple continents due to similar climate conditions)
assessmentOpportunities: Have students create a biome identification key based on temperature and precipitation ranges
Ask students to predict where new biomes would form if climate conditions changed
Challenge students to explain why Australia has unique biome distributions compared to similar latitudes elsewhere
